title: Garlicky Gooey Algerian Lentil Tagine
description: A hearty, aromatic tagine brimming with lentils, sweet palm sugar, and a silky coconut milk finish. Perfect for a comforting dinner that showcases classic Algerian flavors with a modern, Jain-friendly twist.
instructions: Rinse the lentils and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a tagine or heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat.
Add the cumin seeds and let them sizzle until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add the minced garlic, carrots, and zucchini; sauté for 4–5 minutes until the vegetables start to soften.
Stir in the cherry tomatoes and palm sugar, cooking for another 2 minutes.
Add the rinsed lentils and pour in the vegetable broth. Bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low, then stir in the ground cumin, ground coriander, and a pinch of cayenne pepper if desired.
Cover and simmer uncovered for 20–25 minutes, or until the lentils are tender and the liquid has thickened.
Stir in the coconut milk, allowing the mixture to reheat and become silky and gooey; simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
Taste and season with salt to preference. If you’re following strict Jain guidelines, omit the garlic or replace it with a small amount of garlic-infused oil.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs such as cilantro or parsley if desired. Enjoy the warm, aromatic flavors of this Algerian classic.
